title: Validate and parameterize every untrusted input
description: Treat all external input as hostile and constrain it at the trust boundary with allowlists and parameterized APIs before it reaches a sink.
body: Use when handling any request body, query param, header, file, or upstream-service payload that crosses a trust boundary. Do not pass raw input into SQL, shell, template, path, or deserialization sinks, and avoid denylist filtering that attackers route around. Actions: validate type/length/range/format against an allowlist at the edge, then use parameterized queries, prepared statements, or escaping APIs at each sink; record which fields are validated where. Validation signals include negative tests with injection payloads that are rejected and a schema/contract test on the input shape. Risk boundary: if input flows to a sink with no parameterization available, escalate rather than hand-rolling escaping.
